hero.bio: I decode the city through data science. I write code so that urban planning stops relying on intuition and starts listening, with evidence, to the people who inhabit the territory.

viajaSegura.cardDescription1: Converting analog transit records from Mexico City's southern periphery into structured data for technical service validation.
viajaSegura.cardDescription2: The analysis utilizes ETL workflows and GIS modeling to link mobility patterns with urban care facilities, providing data-driven evidence for strategic planning.
viajaSegura.subtitle: CARE-BASED MOBILITY
viajaSegura.title: SAFE JOURNEY
viajaSegura.role: Transit service legitimation through geospatial evidence

proposito.title: 01. Purpose
proposito.content: The project evaluates the operation of 'Route 66', an exclusive service for women and children in CDMX's southern periphery. The challenge involved translating analog social operations into a structured data model to support its relevance before SEMOVI. The goal was to build the operational groundwork required to legitimize the model and project its expansion via technical evidence.

metodologia.title: 02. Structure & Methodology
metodologia.etlText: Design of digital capture instruments to normalize boarding records. Python (GeoPandas) is used for data cleaning and structuring the primary database, resolving georeferencing inconsistencies.
metodologia.spatialText: In GIS, accessibility models were generated using 500m walking isochrones. The analysis automates the overlap of transit supply with care infrastructure, correlating high-flow nodes with facility locations.

insights.title: 03. Insights & Visualization
insights.intro: The result is a tool that transforms coordinates and raw data into strategic decisions:
insights.ejeText: Connects high-altitude peripheries (Oyamel, Ocotal, Antigua) with regional hubs like Ciudad Universitaria.
insights.valText: Legitimation of the route as a vital link in the care mobility network.
insights.visText: Interactive dashboard highlighting the care economy for political advocacy.

stack.title: Tech Stack
stack.content: Python (GeoPandas), QGIS, React, Mapbox GL JS, Figma.

scorecards.rutas_sub: Connecting homes with schools and health services

vigilancia.title: SPECTRAL WATCH
vigilancia.subtitle: Environmental Pressure & Coastal Access Audit
vigilancia.role: Machine Learning Segmentation & Ecosystem Pressure Index
vigilancia.cardDescription1: Machine Learning: Environmental auditing via supervised satellite imagery classification to quantify anthropogenic pressure on mangrove ecosystems in Dorado, Puerto Rico.
vigilancia.cardDescription2: The analysis employs multispectral indices (NDVI/NDWI) to validate segmentation integrity and demonstrates coastal confinement through advanced geospatial modeling.

proposito.title: 01. Purpose
proposito.content: The project consists of an environmental audit in Dorado, Puerto Rico, to quantify the impact generated by resort development on the mangrove ecosystem. The goal is to document vegetation loss and ecosystem health by building a pressure index, transforming satellite observation into technical evidence for evaluation.

metodologia.title: 02. Structure & Methodology
metodologia.intro: The analysis integrates machine learning and multispectral validation to ensure finding accuracy:
metodologia.item1Text: Implementation of a supervised classification model to segment land use into four categories: Urban, Real Estate Investment (resorts), Mangroves, and Sea.
metodologia.item2Text: Vegetation (NDVI) and water (NDWI) indices are generated to perform a cross-validation with the Machine Learning model results.
metodologia.item3Text: Development of a metric to evaluate the level of fragmentation and pressure on the mangrove. This index integrates surrounding infrastructure density and spectral response.

insights.title: 03. Insights & Visualization
insights.intro: The study reveals critical data on territory configuration and raises a hypothesis on coastal accessibility:
insights.item1Text: The analysis reveals a high surface area of real estate investment, confirming land commodification.
insights.item2Text: The combination of architectural and natural barriers may be nullifying public access to beaches guaranteed by law.
insights.item3Text: Interactive visualization that allows contrasting the segmentation model with environmental pressure indices.

stack.title: Tech Stack
stack.content: Python (Scikit-learn, GeoPandas), Google Earth Engine, SNAP, Mapbox GL JS, Figma.

digitaltwin.subtitle: SPATIAL INFERENCE IN CHONGQING
digitaltwin.title: 2.5D DIGITAL TWIN
digitaltwin.role: Urban scale modeling inferred from urban variables

proposito.title: 01. Purpose
proposito.content: This project develops a geointelligence engine based on Digital Twins to infer the city's construction scale. The goal is to model the vertical density of 16,606 parcels based on the weight of tangible, normalized, and weighted urban variables, allowing the visualization of a territory's development potential. Chongqing was selected as the test environment due to its extreme mountainous morphology. The main challenge was to make the algorithm account for the location's contextual restrictions through a Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (MCDA), demonstrating how the city finds space to continue developing despite geographic constraints; a phenomenon validated by confirming that density driven by activity closely matches the satellite light footprint.

metodologia.title: 02. Structure & Methodology
metodologia.intro: The inference engine executes the following logical flow:
metodologia.item1Text: Web scraping of base urban infrastructures (transport nodes, building footprints, road networks, and POIs) using APIs and Python scripts.
metodologia.item2Text: Execution of topological calculations, measuring real distance to main roads and topographic friction towards transit nodes.
metodologia.item3Text: Assignment of geometric scores and parametric normalization of variables.
metodologia.item4Text: Extrusion of architectural volumes dictated by theoretical densification pressure.
metodologia.item5Text: Download of VIIRS satellite spectrometry (nighttime lights) from Google Earth Engine as an empirical reference.

insights.title: 03. Insights & Visualization
insights.intro: The inference model reveals critical spatial behaviors regarding vertical density:
insights.item1Text: Both the spatial model and the VIIRS index show an aggressive densification peak at the convergence of the Jialing and Yangtze rivers, matching the real-world Jiangbeizui financial district.
insights.item2Text: Maximum inferred verticality exists exclusively within a 150-200m radius of a transit node. Beyond walkable limits (500m), scale decreases drastically against topographic friction.
insights.item3Text: Because the base fabric and skyscrapers require almost the same road capacity, the model justifies tower levels only through a combination of perfect topographic access and a hyper-concentration of commercial points.

stack.title: Tech Stack
stack.content: Python | GeoPandas | SciPy | Rasterio | NumPy | GEE API | OSMnx | Overture Maps | PostgreSQL | Supabase | React | Mapbox GL JS | Recharts | QGIS | Figma

factorEsfuerzo.title: EFFORT FACTOR
factorEsfuerzo.subtitle: RAILWAY NETWORK ACCESSIBILITY
factorEsfuerzo.role: Proximity and territorial fragmentation modeling through data mining
factorEsfuerzo.cardDescription1: Data Analysis: Proximity and territorial fragmentation modeling within the Japanese rail network to quantify the 'Hidden Cost' of Shinkansen efficiency.
factorEsfuerzo.cardDescription2: This project delivers a spatial data mining model to identify accessibility gaps in historical heritage sites disconnected from primary railway hubs.

proposito.title: 01. Purpose
proposito.content: The project evaluates the spatial relationship between the Japanese railway network and regional cultural assets. The goal is to quantify the access inequality experienced by points of interest (POIs) not integrated into main nodes, determining the Effort Factor: the physical distance a user must invest from the nearest station to the cultural destination.

metodologia.title: 02. Structure & Methodology
metodologia.item1Text: Development of Python scripts (BeautifulSoup) for web scraping specialized sites, normalizing coordinates and attributes of strategic assets into a structured dataset.
metodologia.item2Text: Automated capture and filtering of the entire Japanese railway network using geospatial plugins for integration into the accessibility model.
metodologia.item3Text: Execution of a proximity analysis in GIS to categorize the difficulty level of access and the relative isolation of peripheral assets.

insights.title: 03. Insights & Visualization
insights.item1Text: It is identified that the infrastructure prioritizes dense commercial nodes, critically increasing the effort to access historical heritage.
insights.item2Text: Detection of clusters where travel effort exceeds standard walkability radii (1km), highlighting connectivity gaps.
insights.item3Text: Thematic visualization correlating station density with POI locations for territorial decision-making.

outro.titulo: READY TO CODE THE NEXT CITY
